title: pittosporum saxicola
intro: Pittosporum saxicola, also known as the rock orohi or mountain orohi, is a small, evergreen shrub native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Pittosporum saxicola are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
